title: Ageratina pazcuarensis
taxonomy: Ageratina pazcuarensis belongs to the family Asteraceae. This plant is a species of flowering plant. It is native to Mexico and Central America. The classification of Ageratina pazcuarensis is as follows: Kingdom: Plantae, Clade: Angiosperms, Clade: Eudicots, Clade: Asterids, Order: Asterales, Family: Asteraceae, Genus: Ageratina, Species: A. pazcuarensis. Further classification and details on this plant are still being researched.
uses: Ageratina pazcuarensis has been traditionally used in folk medicine for various purposes. Its leaves and stems are used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues. It is also worth noting that traditional uses of the plant may not be supported by scientific evidence.
